page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor from the ARMv9 family running at 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration provides 1.0 thread per core and includes a cache layout of 64 KB L1D, 2 MB L2, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, with read-only large block speeds reaching 435.08 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 216.67 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core scores. The instance also excels in database operations and static web serving, achieving top-tier ratings in Passmark database tests and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and Passmark CPU scores align with average tier expectations, highlighting a strong orientation toward parallelized, multi-core workloads.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gn.12xlarge leverages the ARM64 Graviton architecture to deliver high resource density and exceptional memory bandwidth per resource unit. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is supported by the high baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. This hardware profile is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-to-large scale LLM inference text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical ARM64 cores utilizing the 2.8 GHz AWS Neoverse-V2 processor, paired with 192.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core CPU execution. Single-core performance and single-threaded compression remain in the average tier. Qualitatively, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and memory throughput, making this instance highly efficient for parallelized workloads. It is well suited for database operations, multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-to-large scale LLM inference text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 48 dedicated cores, 192 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering high-bandwidth network performance and exceptional multi-core efficiency for parallelized enterprise workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory with top-tier memory bandwidth
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS
Top-tier performance in database operations and software compilation
Suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and LLM inference
